checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. While it contains significant omissions due to an explicit 'token budget' limit in the pipeline, the text that is present corresponds directly to the source document.

There is no fabricated contact information in the output. Many contact details (URLs, phone numbers, emails) present in the source PDF (especially on pages 4, 5, and 28) were omitted because the entire pages/sections were dropped, but none were invented or hallucinated.

The output contains an explicit note that '28 sections omitted for token budget'. This has resulted in the loss of approximately 50% of the document content. Entirely missing pages include 4 through 12, 22, 24, 28, 31, 33, 35, 36, 37, 42, 47, 49, 50, 52, and 53. Significant sections omitted include 'NCDIT's Role', 'Language Access Efforts', the entire 'Definitions' section, 'Factor 1' and 'Factor 2' standards/practices (partially), and 'Section 4: Notification of Language Assistance Services'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source contains 55 pages of content including specific sections like 'Definitions' (pp. 9-11), 'Authority' (p. 7), and 'Section 4' (pp. 41-42).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON explicitly states: '_omitted': '28 sections omitted for token budget'. It jumps from element 21 (page 3) to element 127 (page 13), skipping pages 4-12 entirely. Similar jumps occur throughout.

checks.a_analysis: For the tables that were included in the output, the row counts are accurate compared to the source PDF. For example, the large demographic table on page 54 contains all 12 language data rows in the JSON (element 544). Tables missing due to page/section omissions are covered under C3.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Several sections present in the output are missing substantial content. On page 3, the numbered list 'Overview of Standard' ends after item 2, missing items 3 and 4 which appear on page 4 of the source. On page 44, the 'Implementation Goals' list is missing the first 11 bullet points.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3: 'This Language Access Plan outlines... 3. Implementation Goals... 4. 5-Year Action Plan...'. Page 44: 'Implementation Goals... Review of federal... Review of digital equity... Overview of the value... [total 11 bullets]'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 19 stops after item 2. Element index 460 begins with bullets from page 45 ('Ensure that all new staff...'), omitting the entire list from page 44.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant text truncation in the section regarding interpretation waivers. The name of the guidance document referenced is cut off mid-sentence, leaving the text incomplete and the information missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: ...must sign an interpretation waiver form following the guidelines set in the Proposed Language Access Policy and Interpretation Waiver Form Guidance document.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 297: '...must sign an interpretation waiver form following the guidelines set in the Proposed Language Access Policy and ' element 299: ' document.'

checks.a_analysis: The output contains repeated text strings for the document header ('N.C. Department of Information Technology Language Access Plan') at the beginning of various elements (e.g., elements 8, 127, 139). However, this text appears as a header on every page of the source PDF. Per the instructions, intentional repetition in the source like headers or footers does not constitute a duplication defect.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Major section headers like 'SECTION 1' (element 13) are H2. However, sub-headers within the 'Four-Factor Analysis' (2.1) such as 'Implementation Goals' (element 129) and 'Five-Year Action Plan' (element 134) are also assigned H2. Furthermore, 'Overview of Standard' (element 141) is H2. This creates a flat hierarchy where subsections are at the same level as main sections, breaking screen reader navigation logic.
checks.b_evidence_source: 2.1 FOUR-FACTOR ANALYSIS (Heading); Implementation Goals (Sub-heading); Five-Year Action Plan (Sub-heading)
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION AND BACKGROUND", "level": 2; "text": "Implementation Goals", "level": 2; "text": "Five-Year Action Plan", "level": 2

checks.a_analysis: Tables that span across pages in the source PDF are corrupted in the output. For example, the Factor 1 Action Items table starts on page 13 and ends on page 14. In the output, this is rendered as two separate tables: element 137 contains only the header row, and element 140 contains the data rows but lacks the header row. This occurs for multiple action plan tables throughout the document, making the data tables structurally incomplete.
checks.b_evidence_source: Five-Year Action Plan table starting on page 13 ('Phase', 'Action Items' headers) and continuing onto page 14.
checks.b_evidence_output: Table at element 137 contains headers only. Table at element 140 contains data rows ('II', 'Language Access Coordinator will request...') but lacks the header row.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the logical sequence of the source document. While some elements like the logo on the title page are placed at the end of the section (element 7), this is a minor adjustment for accessibility and does not disrupt the flow of the document content.

Lists are generally identified correctly. For example, the list of implementation goals on page 13 is correctly rendered as an unordered list in element 132. Minor formatting changes like bolding entire list items are observed but the list meaning is preserved.

The output fails to preserve URLs as functional links. URLs present in footnotes (elements 20, 21) are rendered as plain text. Superscript numbers used for footnotes in the text (e.g., element 16) are text-only and not linked to the footnote elements. This violates the requirement to preserve contact and government URLs as functional links.
checks.b_evidence_source: Footnote 1 text on page 3 containing reference to American Community Survey data.
checks.b_evidence_output: "¹Please see Appendix 1: Factor 1 American Community Survey Demographic Data..."

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully maintains the visual organization and logical flow of the original PDF document. Sections, subsections, and tables are presented in the same order as the source. Information is grouped consistently, allowing a user to find content in the same relative locations. The conversion from a paginated PDF to a single-page scrolling HTML document is handled well without compromising the document's structure.

All tables found in the source PDF, including the numerous 'Phase / Action Items' tables and the complex 'Council of Government Regions (COGRs)' ranking table in the Appendix, are correctly rendered as HTML tables. The table structure is intact, with clearly defined rows and columns. Headers are visually distinct, and the content within cells remains legible and properly aligned. There are no instances of broken borders or overlapping text.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the NCDIT logo at the beginning of the document and the map and charts found in the Appendix (Section 9.1). The images are rendered correctly without any broken links or missing elements.

There are no instances of duplicated body content in the HTML output. While headings are listed in the navigation menu/TOC and again in the main body, this is standard practice for accessible web design and does not constitute a defect. Paragraphs and tables are only rendered once in the content area.
